channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: GitHub adds AI-powered bug detection to expand security coverage
item.description: GitHub is adopting AI-based scanning for its Code Security tool to expand vulnerability detections beyond the CodeQL static analysis and cover more languages and frameworks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: At RSAC, the EU Leads While US Officials Are Sidelined
item.description: While US government sits out this year, EU officials are on the ground in San Francisco leading the conversations on today's top cybersecurity challenges....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: PolyShell attacks target 56% of all vulnerable Magento stores
item.description: Attacks leveraging the 'PolyShell' vulnerability in version 2 of Magento Open Source and Adobe Commerce installations are underway, targeting more than half of all vulnerable stores. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Bubble AI app builder abused to steal Microsoft account credentials
item.description: Threat actors are evading phishing detection in campaigns targeting Microsoft accounts by abusing the no-code app-building platform Bubble to generate and host malicious web apps. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Torg Grabber infostealer malware targets 728 crypto wallets
item.description: A new info-stealing malware called Torg Grabber is stealing sensitive data from 850 browser extensions, more than 700 of them for cryptocurrency wallets. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LeakBase Admin Arrested in Russia Over Massive Stolen Credential Marketplace
item.description: The alleged administrator of the LeakBase cybercrime forum has been arrested by Russian law enforcement authorities, state media reported Thursday. According to TASS and MVD Media, a news website link...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Blame Game: Why Public Cyber Attribution Carries Risks
item.description: Publicly accusing an entity of a cyberattack could have negative consequences that organizations should consider before taking the plunge....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Citrix urges admins to patch NetScaler flaws as soon as possible
item.description: Citrix has patched two NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway vulnerabilities, one of which is very similar to the CitrixBleed and CitrixBleed2 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks in recent years. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Phishers Pose as Palo Alto Networks' Recruiters for Months in Job Scam
item.description: A series of campaigns that began in August aim to defraud job candidates, using psychological tactics and data scraped from LinkedIn profiles....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI Dominates RSAC Innovation Sandbox
item.description: Ten finalists will each have three minutes to make their case for being the most innovative, promising young security company of the year. Geordie AI wins the 2026 contest....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: SANS: Top 5 Most Dangerous New Attack Techniques to Watch
item.description: For the first time, SANS Institute's five top attack techniques all have one thing in common — AI....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Uses Solana Dead Drops to Deliver RAT and Steal Browser, Crypto Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new evolution of the GlassWorm campaign that delivers a multi-stage framework capable of comprehensive data theft and installing a remote access trojan (RAT), ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Why a 'Near Miss' Database Is Key to Improving Information Sharing
item.description: Organizations disclose attack details, though information may be limited, following a breach, but what if they did the same with close calls?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Paid AI Accounts Are Now a Hot Underground Commodity
item.description: AI accounts are becoming part of the cybercrime supply chain, sold like email accounts or VPS access. Flare Systems shows how underground markets bundle and resell premium AI access at scale. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Kali Linux 2026.1 released with 8 new tools, new BackTrack mode
item.description: Kali Linux 2026.1, the first release of the year, is now available for download, featuring 8 new tools, a theme refresh, and a new BackTrack mode for Kali-Undercover. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI-Native Security Is a Must to Counter AI-Based Attacks
item.description: Attacks by artificial intelligence agents are a reality. Experts at Nvidia's GTC conference say defenders need to use the same tools to fight them off....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Kill Chain Is Obsolete When Your AI Agent Is the Threat
item.description: In September 2025, Anthropic disclosed that a state-sponsored threat actor used an AI coding agent to execute an autonomous cyber espionage campaign against 30 global targets. The AI handled 80-90% of...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ex-NSA Directors Discuss 'Red Line' for Offensive Cyberattacks
item.description: Four former NSA chiefs representing a near-complete history of US Cyber Command debated and discussed the role of offensive cyber in the government....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Russian Hacker Sentenced to 2 Years for TA551 Botnet-Driven Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) said a Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison for managing a botnet that was used to launch ransomware attacks against U.S. companies. Ilya Ang...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Device Code Phishing Hits 340+ Microsoft 365 Orgs Across Five Countries via OAuth Abuse
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to an active device code phishing campaign that's targeting Microsoft 365 identities across more than 340 organizations in the U.S., Canada, Australia, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TP-Link warns users to patch critical router auth bypass flaw
item.description: TP-Link has patched several vulnerabilities in its Archer NX router series, including a critical-severity flaw that may allow attackers to bypass authentication and upload new firmware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Manager of botnet used in ransomware attacks gets 2 years in prison
item.description: A Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison after admitting that the phishing botnet he managed was used to launch BitPaymer ransomware attacks against 72 U.S. companies. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FCC Bans New Foreign-Made Routers Over Supply Chain and Cyber Risk Concerns
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said on Monday that it was banning the import of new, foreign-made consumer routers, citing "unacceptable" risks to cyber and national security. The ac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran Hacktivists Make Noise but Have Little Impact on War
item.description: Iran-aligned groups are trying to make their mark in the Gulf, but the results have fallen short of remarkable....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: PTC warns of imminent threat from critical Windchill, FlexPLM RCE bug
item.description: PTC Inc. is warning of a critical vulnerability in Windchill and FlexPLM, widely used product lifecycle management (PLM) solutions, that could allow remote code execution. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CSA Launches CSAI Foundation for AI Security
item.description: The Cloud Security Alliance creates a dedicated nonprofit to govern autonomous AI agent ecosystems through risk intelligence and certification....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Popular LiteLLM PyPI package backdoored to steal credentials, auth tokens
item.description: The TeamPCP hacking group continues its supply-chain rampage, now compromising the massively popular "LiteLLM" Python package on PyPI and claiming to have stolen data from hundreds of thousands of dev...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Checkmarx KICS Code Scanner Targeted in Widening Supply Chain Hit
item.description: TeamPCP is the likely cyber threat actor behind attacks on Trivy, Checkmarx's KICS and VS Code plug-ins, and the LiteLLM AI library — and all signs point to more attacks to come....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: How AI Coding Tools Crushed the Endpoint Security Fortress
item.description: Security vendors have spent years building up defenses around the endpoint, but one researcher says AI coding tools have brought the walls down....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FCC bans new routers made outside the USA over security risks
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has updated its Covered List to include all consumer routers made in foreign countries, banning the sale of new models in the U.S. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
